---
title: "Gnome-ppp works only with &quot;Remember password&quot;"
date: 2006-08-27
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by rogblake on 2006-08-27
I'm finding that gnome-ppp only seems to work if the "Remember password" option is enabled. If this is left unchecked, when attempting to dial out gnome-ppp immediately pops up the message "Sending Password..." (before the modem is even take off-hook), and it just hangs there. The following appears in the log:

--------------------------------------------------------------------
--> Ignoring malformed input line: ";Do NOT edit this file by hand!"
--> WvDial: Internet dialer version 1.55
--> Cannot set information for serial port.
--> Initializing modem.
--> Sending: ATZ
ATZ
OK
--> Sending: ATQ0 V1 E1 S0=0 &C1 &D2 +FCLASS=0
ATQ0 V1 E1 S0=0 &C1 &D2 +FCLASS=0
OK
--> Modem initialized.
--> Please enter password (or empty password to stop):
-------------------------------------------------------------------

So it appears that wvdial is waiting for the password to be entered, this should of course have been passed through to it by gnome-ppp.

Is there a fix or workaround for this problem? (I would prefer *not* to have gnome-ppp storing the password.)

---
